enonce.texte:
Read the text below about a typical morning and answer the questions that follow.

My name is Amina. I live in Douala with my parents. I usually wake up at 5:30 am. I always take a shower before breakfast. I sometimes eat bread and butter, but I often eat rice and beans. I rarely drink coffee; I prefer tea. I never go to school late. On Saturdays, I do housework with my sister. We always sweep the yard and wash the dishes. We sometimes watch TV after cleaning.
images: A cartoon illustration of a young African girl waking up in a sunny bedroom.

questions.texte: List the adverbs of frequency you found in the text.
questions.reponse: usually, always, sometimes, often, rarely, never.

questions.texte: Identify the adverb of frequency that means 'not at any time'.
questions.reponse: never

questions.texte: What does Amina often eat for breakfast?
questions.reponse: She often eats rice and beans.

questions.texte: Based on the text, what is Amina's habit regarding school?
questions.reponse: She never goes to school late.

intitule: Vocabulary and Grammar: Adverbs of Frequency

enonce.texte: Complete the following sentences using the adverbs of frequency from the list: always, usually, sometimes, often, rarely, never.

questions.texte: He _______ brushes his teeth in the morning and at night. (100%)
questions.reponse: always

questions.texte: They _______ eat pizza for dinner; they prefer rice and fish. (0%)
questions.reponse: never

questions.texte: I _______ walk to school, but today I took the bus because it was raining. (about 50%)
questions.reponse: sometimes

questions.texte: We _______ visit our grandmother on Sundays. (about 80%)
questions.reponse: usually

enonce.texte: Write a short paragraph (5-7 sentences) describing your daily routine or habits. You must use the following adverbs of frequency: always, sometimes, often, rarely, never. Your paragraph can be about your morning routine, your chores at home, or your activities after school.

questions.texte: Write a paragraph as described. Ensure you use the required adverbs.
questions.reponse: Example answer: I always wake up at 6:00 am. I often eat bread for breakfast. I rarely play video games in the morning. I sometimes help my mother with the dishes. I never go to school without my books. In the afternoon, I usually do my homework.

intitule: Reading and Comprehension: A Family's Habits

enonce.texte:
Read the text about the Nkeng family and their habits. Then, answer the questions.

The Nkeng family lives in Yaoundé. Mr. Nkeng is a teacher. He always reads the newspaper after breakfast. Mrs. Nkeng is a nurse. She usually leaves for work very early in the morning. Their children, Kevin and Mireille, often watch TV after school. They sometimes eat dinner at 7:00 pm. Mireille always helps set the table. Kevin rarely helps with the dishes. The family never goes to bed before 10:00 pm.
images: An illustration of a happy African family sitting around a dining table.

questions.texte: What does Mr. Nkeng always do after breakfast?
questions.reponse: He always reads the newspaper.

questions.texte: Describe Mrs. Nkeng's morning habit.
questions.reponse: She usually leaves for work very early in the morning.

questions.texte: Find two adverbs of frequency that mean 'not often' in the text.
questions.reponse: rarely (and sometimes)

questions.texte: Explain the difference in the children's habits regarding household chores.
questions.reponse: Mireille always helps set the table, but Kevin rarely helps with the dishes. This means Mireille is more helpful in the kitchen than Kevin.

enonce.texte: Write a short text (8-10 sentences) describing the daily habits of one of your family members or a friend. You should mention their routine and how often they do certain things.
images: A simple illustration of a student writing in a notebook.

questions.texte: Describe the daily habits and routines of a person you know, using a variety of adverbs of frequency.
questions.reponse: Example answer: My mother always wakes up at 5:00 am to pray. She usually goes to the market in the morning. She often cooks lunch for the family. She sometimes buys new clothes for us on weekends. She rarely eats outside; she prefers home-cooked food. She never forgets to prepare my uniform for school. She always reads a book before sleeping. She is a very organized person.

intitule: Listening and Speaking: Interview About Habits

enonce.texte: Role-play: In pairs, conduct a short interview. One student is a journalist, and the other is a famous Cameroonian athlete. The journalist asks about the athlete's daily habits, training schedule, and diet. Use adverbs of frequency in your questions and answers.

questions.texte: Write a short dialogue between you and your partner for this role-play.
questions.reponse:
Example dialogue:

Journalist: Hello, champion! How do you start your day?

Athlete: I always wake up at 5:00 am for my morning run.

Journalist: Do you eat a big breakfast?

Athlete: I often eat bananas and eggs. I rarely eat heavy food in the morning.

Journalist: What about your training? Do you train every day?

Athlete: I usually train twice a day, but I sometimes take a rest on Sundays.

Journalist: Do you ever eat unhealthy food?

Athlete: I never eat junk food. It is not good for my health.

Journalist: Thank you for your time!

enonce.texte: Put the words in the correct order to form meaningful sentences about habits and routines.

questions.texte: go / I / school / always / to / on time
questions.reponse: I always go to school on time.

questions.texte: my / helps / rarely / father / mother / the / with / cooking
questions.reponse: My father rarely helps my mother with the cooking.

questions.texte: in / our / never / we / the / yard / play / evening
questions.reponse: We never play in the yard in the evening.

questions.texte: sometimes / goes / to / she / the / Saturday / on / cinema
questions.reponse: She sometimes goes to the cinema on Saturday.

enonce.texte: Imagine you are a young student. Write a diary entry for a week (Monday to Sunday) describing what you do each day. Use at least five different adverbs of frequency to describe your habits and routines during the week.

questions.texte: Write a detailed diary entry covering your activities from Monday to Sunday.
questions.reponse:
Example answer:

Monday: I always have a Maths class; I find it difficult but fun. Tuesday: I often play football after school with my friends. Wednesday: I sometimes go to the library to read. Thursday: I usually have a lot of homework. Friday: I rarely watch TV at night; I prefer to go to bed early. Saturday: I always help my mother clean the house. Sunday: I never go out; I rest at home.
